1142825.courseName: IFT 530: Adv DB Management Systems (2025 Spring C)
1142825.title: Module 3: Populating Tables Quiz
1142825.description: Test your understanding of techniques and concepts related to populating tables in advanced database management systems.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is the primary method for inserting data into a table in SQL?
options: CREATE TABLE statement
INSERT INTO statement
SELECT statement
DROP TABLE statement
questions.explanation: The INSERT INTO statement is the standard SQL command used to add new rows of data to an existing table, as shown in the 'Populating Tables' sample code.

questions.prompt: When bulk populating a table, what is a common advantage of using a single INSERT statement with multiple rows over many individual INSERT statements?
options: It automatically validates the data types
It reduces transaction overhead and improves performance
It requires less disk space
It prevents duplicates automatically
questions.explanation: Using a single INSERT with multiple rows reduces transaction overhead by committing once, enhancing performance, which is a common best practice in populating tables.

questions.prompt: In the context of populating tables, what is a commonly recommended practice when inserting data to ensure data integrity?
options: Disable all constraints before inserting data
Use transactions to group multiple insertions
Insert data without specifying column names to save typing
Use SELECT * to copy data between tables
questions.explanation: Grouping multiple insertions within transactions ensures either all data is committed or none, maintaining data integrity during population as suggested in assignment guidelines.

questions.prompt: Which option best describes the use of the SELECT statement in populating tables?
options: It can be used within an INSERT to populate data from another table
It is used exclusively for querying data, not inserting
It automatically populates tables with default values
It creates new tables from query results
questions.explanation: The INSERT INTO ... SELECT statement allows populating a table by copying data from another table, an efficient technique covered in example code.

questions.prompt: What is an important consideration when writing sample code to populate tables in assignments for this course?
options: Use dummy data with clear structure and comments
Avoid specifying column names in INSERT statements
Write code without transaction control for simplicity
Use only single row insert statements
questions.explanation: Writing clear, well-structured sample code with comments and dummy data helps in understanding and maintaining the code, as emphasized in the assignment and sample code materials.

questions.prompt: Why might you choose to disable indexes before bulk inserting data into a table during population?
options: Because indexes improve insertion speed
To avoid index maintenance overhead during bulk load
Because disabling indexes prevents duplicate rows
Disabling indexes is required for all insertions
questions.explanation: Disabling indexes temporarily during bulk inserts avoids the overhead of updating indexes for each row, thereby improving load performance, a strategy sometimes used in advanced population scenarios.

1142825.courseName: IFT 530: Adv DB Management Systems (2025 Spring C)
1142825.title: Module 5: Scripting Quiz
1142825.description: Test your understanding of scripting concepts and sample code covered in Module 5.

questions.prompt: What is the primary purpose of scripting in advanced database management systems as discussed in this module?
options: To design database schemas
To automate routine database tasks
To create user interfaces
To perform hardware diagnostics
questions.explanation: Scripting in database management focuses on automating routine tasks such as backups, data import/export, and maintenance, as outlined in the module material.

questions.prompt: Which scripting language feature is emphasized in the sample code provided in the module to handle conditional logic?
options: For loops
If-else statements
Switch-case statements
Try-catch blocks
questions.explanation: The sample code highlights using if-else statements for implementing conditional logic, allowing scripts to execute different commands based on certain criteria.

questions.prompt: According to the scripting assignment guidelines, what is a critical aspect when writing scripts for database automation?
options: Minimizing user input
Ensuring the script runs on multiple database platforms
Writing detailed comments and documentation
Maximizing the use of global variables
questions.explanation: The assignment stresses the importance of writing clear comments and documentation to maintain script clarity and ease future maintenance.

questions.prompt: Based on the module content, what is the recommended practice to handle errors in scripting for database operations?
options: Ignoring errors to speed up execution
Logging errors and implementing exception handling
Restarting the database server automatically
Using nested loops to isolate errors
questions.explanation: The module recommends logging errors and using proper exception handling to ensure robustness and easier debugging of scripts.

questions.prompt: In the sample scripting code, which method is demonstrated for passing parameters to the script?
options: Reading from a configuration file
Using command-line arguments
Hardcoding values within the script
Using environment variables
questions.explanation: The sample code showcases passing parameters via command-line arguments to allow flexible and reusable script execution.

questions.prompt: What is one advantage of scripting emphasized in the Module 5 learning materials over manual database management?
options: Scripts require no technical knowledge
Scripts can execute tasks faster and consistently
Scripts automatically optimize database queries
Scripts eliminate the need for database backups
questions.explanation: The learning materials highlight that scripts enable faster execution of repetitive tasks with consistency, reducing human error.

1142825.courseName: DSE 501: Statistics for Data Analysts (2025 Spring C)
1142825.title: DSE 501: Statistics for Data Analysts (2025 Spring C) Custom Quiz
1142825.description: Test your understanding of fundamental concepts in probability, random variables, and distributions.

questions.prompt: Which of the following statements correctly distinguishes mutually exclusive events from independent events?
options: Mutually exclusive events always satisfy P(A|B) = P(A), while independent events satisfy A ∩ B = ∅.
Mutually exclusive events have no intersection meaning A ∩ B = ∅, whereas independence means P(A|B) = P(A).
Independence means the events have no intersection, and mutually exclusive means P(A|B) = P(A).
Mutually exclusive and independent events are always the same.
questions.explanation: Mutually exclusive events have no intersection (A ∩ B = ∅), while independent events satisfy that the conditional probability is equal to the marginal (P(A|B) = P(A)).

questions.prompt: If X is a continuous random variable with PDF f_X(x) and CDF F_X(x), which of the following is true?
options: F_X(x) = P(X ≥ x) and f_X(x) is the derivative of F_X(x) with respect to x.
F_X(x) = P(X ≤ x) and f_X(x) = d/dx F_X(x).
P(a ≤ X ≤ b) = F_X(a) - F_X(b) for any a < b.
For a continuous variable, P(X = x) = 1.
questions.explanation: By definition, the CDF is F_X(x) = P(X ≤ x) and the PDF is the derivative of the CDF, f_X(x) = d/dx F_X(x). Probability at a single point for continuous variables is zero.

questions.prompt: The Law of Total Probability is crucial in Bayesian inference. Which formula correctly expresses this law when {A_i} partitions the sample space?
options: P(B) = Σ_i P(A_i ∩ B)
P(B) = Σ_i P(B | A_i) P(A_i)
P(A_i | B) = P(B | A_i) P(A_i) / P(B)
P(A_i) = P(B | A_i) P(B)
questions.explanation: The Law of Total Probability states P(B) = Σ_i P(B | A_i) P(A_i) where the {A_i} are mutually exclusive and cover the whole sample space.

questions.prompt: Consider a discrete random variable X with E[X] = μ and Var(X) = σ². Which property correctly describes how variance changes under a linear transformation Y = aX + b?
options: Var(Y) = a Var(X) + b
Var(Y) = a² Var(X)
Var(Y) = Var(X) + b²
Var(Y) = a Var(X) - b
questions.explanation: Variance scales by the square of the constant multiplied to the variable: Var(aX + b) = a² Var(X). Adding b shifts the mean but does not affect variance.

questions.prompt: Which of the following statements correctly describes the unbiasedness property of the sample variance S² estimator?
options: E[S²] = Var(X) because S² divides by n correcting the bias.
E[S²] = Var(X) because S² divides by n - 1 (Bessel’s correction) to correct the bias.
E[S²] > Var(X) because S² always overestimates variance.
E[S²] < Var(X) because sample variance underestimates population variance.
questions.explanation: Sample variance S² uses divisor n - 1 (Bessel’s correction) which makes it an unbiased estimator: E[S²] = Var(X).

questions.prompt: Given a Bernoulli random variable X with P(X = 1) = p, which distribution describes the number of failures before the first success?
options: Binomial distribution
Geometric distribution
Negative Binomial distribution
Poisson distribution
questions.explanation: The Geometric distribution models the number of failures before the first success in Bernoulli trials.

questions.prompt: Suppose we transform a standard normal variable X ~ N(0,1) using Y = exp(X). Which statement about the distribution of Y is correct?
options: Y has a normal distribution because exponentiation preserves normality.
Y has a log-normal distribution since Y = exp(X) and X is normal.
Y has a uniform distribution on (0,1).
Y has a Poisson distribution due to the transformation.
questions.explanation: Exponentiating a normal variable produces a log-normal distribution, i.e., Y = exp(X) when X ~ N(0,1).

1142825.courseName: IFT 530: Adv DB Management Systems (2025 Spring C)
1142825.title: Module 6: Stored Procedures Quiz
1142825.description: This quiz tests your understanding of stored procedures concepts, usage, and implementation from the Module 6 materials.

questions.prompt: What is the primary advantage of using stored procedures in a database system?
options: They eliminate the need for transactions
They allow logic execution on the server for improved performance
They automatically optimize complex queries for the user
They store data backups within the database
questions.explanation: Stored procedures execute on the database server, which reduces client-server communication and improves performance, a key benefit noted in the Overview of Stored Procedures.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a typical feature supported by stored procedures as described in the course materials?
options: User interface rendering
Conditional logic and control flow
Client-side scripting
Automatic schema migration
questions.explanation: Stored procedures can include conditional logic and control flow (such as IF statements and loops), enabling more complex server-side operations, as highlighted in the Learning Materials on Stored Procedures.

questions.prompt: When invoking a stored procedure with parameters, how are input and output parameters generally handled?
options: Input parameters are required, output parameters are optional and allow returning values
Only output parameters are allowed, input parameters are forbidden
Parameters must all be of the same data type
Stored procedures cannot accept any parameters
questions.explanation: Stored procedures typically accept input parameters to pass data in and can have output parameters to return data, as illustrated in the SP Sample Code material.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes how stored procedures contribute to database security?
options: Stored procedures automatically encrypt all data
They limit direct table access by encapsulating queries behind controlled execution
Stored procedures remove the need for database user authentication
They disable all external database connections
questions.explanation: By encapsulating SQL statements inside stored procedures, direct table access can be restricted, enhancing security through controlled execution paths, as explained in the Overview and Learning Materials.

questions.prompt: In the context of the provided Assignment on Stored Procedures, why is error handling within a stored procedure important?
options: It guarantees the procedure runs infinitely
It helps detect and manage exceptions, maintaining data integrity
It automatically fixes all database errors
It disables transaction rollbacks
questions.explanation: Proper error handling allows procedures to detect exceptions and handle them gracefully, which is essential for maintaining data integrity, as emphasized in the Assignment instructions.

questions.prompt: Looking at the SP Sample Code, which SQL statement is commonly used to create a stored procedure?
options: CREATE FUNCTION
CREATE PROCEDURE
CREATE VIEW
CREATE TRIGGER
questions.explanation: The standard syntax to create a stored procedure uses the CREATE PROCEDURE statement, as shown in the sample code provided in the Learning Materials.

1142825.courseName: IFT 530: Adv DB Management Systems (2025 Spring C)
1142825.title: IFT 530: Adv DB Management Systems (2025 Spring C) Custom Quiz
1142825.description: Assess understanding of SQL queries, views, and triggers from selected modules.

questions.prompt: What is the primary purpose of a SQL view in database management?
options: To store data physically in a new table
To provide a virtual table that encapsulates a query
To enforce referential integrity between tables
To trigger automatic actions after data modification
questions.explanation: Views act as virtual tables that encapsulate query results without storing the data physically, simplifying data access as discussed in Module 4 Queries and Views.

questions.prompt: Which type of SQL query would most likely be used to define a view?
options: SELECT query
INSERT query
UPDATE query
DELETE query
questions.explanation: A view is defined using a SELECT query that specifies the virtual table's contents, as shown in the learning materials on Queries and Views in Module 4.

questions.prompt: How do triggers differ from standard SQL queries?
options: Triggers store results in new tables automatically
Triggers execute automatically in response to data changes
Triggers are only used for creating views
Triggers cannot modify data
questions.explanation: Triggers automatically execute predefined actions in response to data changes, like INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE events, as outlined in Module 9 on Triggers.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a valid use case for database triggers based on Module 9 materials?
options: Materializing a view for faster querying
Automatically validating and enforcing complex business rules during data updates
Creating backups of the database regularly
Executing ad-hoc SELECT queries on demand
questions.explanation: Triggers are used to enforce complex business rules automatically during data modifications, as described in the Triggers module.

questions.prompt: In the context of SQL views, which statement is true regarding updates?
options: All views are inherently updatable without restrictions
Views that involve joins are always updatable
Some views can be updated if the DBMS supports it and certain conditions are met
Views can never be used to update underlying tables
questions.explanation: While many views are read-only, some views can be updatable if conditions such as a single base table and key preservation are met, as indicated in the Queries and Views materials.

questions.prompt: When defining a trigger, what events typically cause a trigger to fire as per the Module 9 overview?
options: Query compilation only
Data definition changes like ALTER TABLE only
Data modification events like INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE
User login and logout events
questions.explanation: Triggers are configured to fire automatically on data modification events such as INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE operations, according to the triggers overview in Module 9.

1142825.courseName: CSE 512: Distributed Database Systems (2025 Fall C)
1142825.title: CSE 512: Distributed Database Systems (2025 Fall C) Custom Quiz
1142825.description: Quiz covering fragmentation, partitioning, and TPC-H dataset usage in PostgreSQL.

questions.prompt: What is the primary motivation for using fragmentation and partitioning in large databases such as those modeled by the TPC-H benchmark?
options: To reduce database size by deleting old records
To improve performance by reducing query scan times and managing backups efficiently
To simplify schema design by merging tables
To ensure ACID compliance in distributed transactions
questions.explanation: Fragmentation and partitioning help scale large databases by reducing query runtime scanning millions of rows and making backup and indexing operations more manageable, as emphasized in the DDS CS02.pdf notes on TPC-H.

questions.prompt: In the TPC-H benchmark implemented on PostgreSQL, which two tables typically grow very large and thus benefit most from fragmentation or partitioning?
options: Customer and Supplier
Nation and Region
Lineitem and Orders
Part and Partsupp
questions.explanation: The Lineitem and Orders tables are noted as large and ideal for performance demonstrations, motivating fragmentation/partitioning to handle scale effectively.

questions.prompt: Which type of fragmentation involves dividing a table by selecting subsets of rows based on predicates, as discussed in the context of TPC-H fragmentation?
options: Horizontal fragmentation
Vertical fragmentation
Hybrid fragmentation
Hash fragmentation
questions.explanation: Horizontal fragmentation divides a table into subsets of rows by predicates, a method detailed in the DDS CS02.pdf slides under fragmentation alternatives.

questions.prompt: In PostgreSQL, what partitioning method is most similar to the fragmentation techniques studied in distributed database systems, particularly when applied to the TPC-H dataset?
options: Vertical partitioning using column-store tables
PostgreSQL table partitioning, such as range, list, and hash partitioning
Using foreign data wrappers
Materialized views
questions.explanation: PostgreSQL table partitioning methods (range, list, hash) implement fragmentation-like data division, helping simulate distributed fragmentation as covered in the DDS CS02.pdf slides.

questions.prompt: Why is the PostgreSQL command ANALYZE recommended after bulk loading the TPC-H data before running queries?
options: To create indexes on all tables automatically
To clean and sanitize the data imported from CSV files
To build and refresh optimizer statistics for more efficient query planning
To reorganize tables physically on disk for faster access
questions.explanation: ANALYZE updates PostgreSQL's internal statistics that help the query planner pick efficient execution plans, essential for fair baseline timings and query performance as described in TPC_H_Data_into_PgAdmin+Queries+Solution.pdf.

questions.prompt: In the SQL examples using the TPC-H dataset, what is the difference between JOIN and NATURAL JOIN?
options: JOIN combines tables only if they have no common columns; NATURAL JOIN always causes a cross product
JOIN requires explicitly specifying join columns; NATURAL JOIN automatically joins on all common column names
JOIN is deprecated and replaced by NATURAL JOIN in SQL standards
JOIN filters rows based on conditions; NATURAL JOIN does not filter rows
questions.explanation: A JOIN requires specifying join conditions, while NATURAL JOIN automatically matches and joins using all columns with the same names, as explained in the pgAdmin TPC-H SQL queries document.

questions.prompt: What does the EXCEPT operator do in a query on the TPC-H dataset?
options: Combines two result sets and removes duplicates
Finds common rows between two result sets
Returns all rows from the first query that do not appear in the second query
Sorts the results of a query in ascending order
questions.explanation: EXCEPT returns the difference between two result sets, selecting rows present in the first query that are absent in the second, as demonstrated in the TPC-H SQL excerpt.

questions.prompt: In TPC-H query optimization on PostgreSQL, why might increasing the session work memory (work_mem) setting be beneficial?
options: It reduces the amount of memory available for connections
It forces queries to use indexes instead of table scans
It helps avoid temporary disk spills during large GROUP BYs, ORDER BYs, and hash joins, improving timing consistency
It disables query parallelism to simplify debugging
questions.explanation: Setting a higher work_mem helps PostgreSQL keep more intermediate data in memory during resource-intensive operations, preventing slower disk spills and making query timings more consistent as recommended in the TPC_H Data document.
